1	import { z } from "zod"
2	
3	// Note: if you edit the schema here, you must also edit the schema in the
4	// apps/v4/public/schema/registry-item.json file.
5	
6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({
65	  resolvedPaths: z.object({
66	    cwd: z.string(),
67	    tailwindConfig: z.string(),
68	    tailwindCss: z.string(),
69	    utils: z.string(),
70	    components: z.string(),
71	    lib: z.string(),
72	    hooks: z.string(),
73	    ui: z.string(),
74	  }),
75	})
76	
77	// TODO: type the key.
78	// Okay for now since I don't want a breaking change.
79	export const workspaceConfigSchema = z.record(configSchema)
80	
81	export const registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([
82	  "registry:lib",
83	  "registry:block",
84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",
94	
95	  // Internal use only.
96	  "registry:example",
97	  "registry:internal",
98	])
99	
100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
101	  // Target is required for registry:file and registry:page
102	  z.object({
103	    path: z.string(),
104	    content: z.string().optional(),
105	    type: z.enum(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
106	    target: z.string(),
107	  }),
108	  z.object({
109	    path: z.string(),
110	    content: z.string().optional(),
111	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
112	    target: z.string().optional(),
113	  }),
114	])
115	
116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({
117	  config: z
118	    .object({
119	      content: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
120	      theme: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
121	      plugins: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
122	    })
123	    .optional(),
124	})
125	
126	export const registryItemCssVarsSchema = z.object({
127	  theme: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
128	  light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([
135	    z.string(),
136	    z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.record(z.string(), z.string())])),
137	    z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema),
138	  ])
139	)
140	
141	export const registryItemCssSchema = z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema)
142	
143	export const registryItemEnvVarsSchema = z.record(z.string(), z.string())
144	
145	// Font metadata schema for registry:font items.
146	export const registryItemFontSchema = z.object({
147	  family: z.string(),
148	  provider: z.literal("google"),
149	  import: z.string(),
150	  variable: z.string(),
151	  weight: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
152	  subsets: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
153	  selector: z.string().optional(),
154	  dependency: z.string().optional(),
155	})
156	
157	// Common fields shared by all registry items.
158	export const registryItemCommonSchema = z.object({
159	  $schema: z.string().optional(),
160	  extends: z.string().optional(),
161	  name: z.string(),
162	  title: z.string().optional(),
163	  author: z.string().min(2).optional(),
164	  description: z.string().optional(),
165	  dependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
166	  devDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
167	  registryDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
168	  files: z.array(registryItemFileSchema).optional(),
169	  tailwind: registryItemTailwindSchema.optional(),
170	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
171	  css: registryItemCssSchema.optional(),
172	  envVars: registryItemEnvVarsSchema.optional(),
173	  meta: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
174	  docs: z.string().optional(),
175	  categories: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
176	})
177	
178	// registry:base has a config field, registry:font has a font field.
179	export const registryItemSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
180	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
181	    type: z.literal("registry:base"),
182	    config: rawConfigSchema.deepPartial().optional(),
183	  }),
184	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
185	    type: z.literal("registry:font"),
186	    font: registryItemFontSchema,
187	  }),
188	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
189	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:base", "registry:font"]),
190	  }),
191	])
192	
193	export type RegistryItem = z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>
194	
195	// Helper type for registry:base items specifically.
196	export type RegistryBaseItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:base" }>
197	
198	// Helper type for registry:font items specifically.
199	export type RegistryFontItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:font" }>
200	
201	export const registrySchema = z.object({
202	  name: z.string(),
203	  homepage: z.string(),
204	  items: z.array(registryItemSchema),
205	})
206	
207	export type Registry = z.infer<typeof registrySchema>
208	
209	export const registryIndexSchema = z.array(registryItemSchema)
210	
211	export const stylesSchema = z.array(
212	  z.object({
213	    name: z.string(),
214	    label: z.string(),
215	  })
216	)
217	
218	export const iconsSchema = z.record(
219	  z.string(),
220	  z.record(z.string(), z.string())
221	)
222	
223	export const registryBaseColorSchema = z.object({
224	  inlineColors: z.object({
225	    light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),
226	    dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),
227	  }),
228	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema,
229	  cssVarsV4: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
230	  inlineColorsTemplate: z.string(),
231	  cssVarsTemplate: z.string(),
232	})
233	
234	export const registryResolvedItemsTreeSchema = registryItemCommonSchema
235	  .pick({
236	    dependencies: true,
237	    devDependencies: true,
238	    files: true,
239	    tailwind: true,
240	    cssVars: true,
241	    css: true,
242	    envVars: true,
243	    docs: true,
244	  })
245	  .extend({
246	    fonts: z
247	      .array(
248	        registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
249	          type: z.literal("registry:font"),
250	          font: registryItemFontSchema,
251	        })
252	      )
253	      .optional(),
254	  })
255	
256	export const searchResultItemSchema = z.object({
257	  name: z.string(),
258	  type: z.string().optional(),
259	  description: z.string().optional(),
260	  registry: z.string(),
261	  addCommandArgument: z.string(),
262	})
263	
264	export const searchResultsSchema = z.object({
265	  pagination: z.object({
266	    total: z.number(),
267	    offset: z.number(),
268	    limit: z.number(),
269	    hasMore: z.boolean(),
270	  }),
271	  items: z.array(searchResultItemSchema),
272	})
273	
274	// Legacy schema for getRegistriesIndex() backward compatibility.
275	export const registriesIndexSchema = z.record(
276	  z.string().regex(/^@[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9-_]*$/),
277	  z.string()
278	)
279	
280	// New schema for getRegistries().
281	export const registriesSchema = z.array(
282	  z.object({
283	    name: z.string(),
284	    homepage: z.string().optional(),
285	    url: z.string(),
286	    description: z.string().optional(),
287	  })
288	)
289	
290	export const presetSchema = z.object({
291	  name: z.string(),
292	  title: z.string(),
293	  description: z.string(),
294	  base: z.string(),
295	  style: z.string(),
296	  baseColor: z.string(),
297	  theme: z.string(),
298	  iconLibrary: z.string(),
299	  font: z.string(),
300	  rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
301	  menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]),
302	  menuColor: z.enum([
303	    "default",
304	    "inverted",
305	    "default-translucent",
306	    "inverted-translucent",
307	  ]),
308	  radius: z.string(),
309	})
310	
311	export type Preset = z.infer<typeof presetSchema>
312	
313	export const configJsonSchema = z.object({
314	  presets: z.array(presetSchema),
315	})
316	
317	export type ConfigJson = z.infer<typeof configJsonSchema>

## Project Structure Overview

This is the **shadcn/ui v4** component library - a sophisticated, monorepo-based design system built on TypeScript, React, Tailwind CSS, and Radix UI. Here's the complete layout:

### Top-Level Directory Structure

```
/
├── apps/                    # Application packages
│   └── v4/                 # Main shadcn/ui v4 Next.js app (documentation & demo site)
├── packages/               # Library packages
│   ├── shadcn/            # Core CLI tool for adding components
│   └── tests/             # Test utilities package
├── templates/              # Template projects for different frameworks
├── scripts/                # Build and utility scripts
├── skills/                 # Custom skills/tools
├── .github/                # GitHub workflows and scripts
├── .changeset/             # Changesets for versioning
└── [Config files]          # Root-level configuration
```

### Key Root-Level Config Files

- **package.json**: Monorepo root (pnpm workspaces, Turbo build setup)
- **tsconfig.json**: TypeScript configuration
- **turbo.json**: Turbo build orchestration (caching, task dependencies, env vars)
- **.eslintrc.json**: ESLint configuration
- **.commitlintrc.json**: Conventional commits validation
- **pnpm-workspace.yaml**: pnpm monorepo definition
- **vitest.workspace.ts**: Vitest test configuration
- **prettier.config.cjs**: Code formatting rules

### apps/v4 - Main Documentation & Demo App

This is a Next.js 16 app with:

**Key Directories:**
- **registry/new-york-v4/**: Complete component registry with subtree:
  - `ui/`: Actual component implementations (~60+ components)
  - `blocks/`: Pre-built component compositions
  - `charts/`: Chart components using Recharts
  - `examples/`: Usage examples for documentation
  - `hooks/`: Custom React hooks
  - `lib/`: Utility functions
  - `internal/`: Internal utilities
  - `registry.ts`: Main registry export file

- **registry/bases/**: Component base definitions for:
  - `base/`: Base UI components
  - `radix/`: Radix UI-based components

- **registry/styles/**: Multiple style variants (vega, nova, maia, lyra, luma, mira) each with CSS files

- **registry/icons/**: Icon library definitions

- **styles/**: Style files for different theme presets (radix-vega, base-luma, etc.)

- **content/docs/**: MDX documentation files
- **components/**: Demo/site-specific components
- **public/r/**: Registry JSON outputs (generated component metadata)
  - `index.json`: Main component registry index
  - `config.json`: Preset configurations (12+ themes combining base styles + icon libraries + fonts)
  - `colors/*.json`: Color palette definitions
  - `styles/*/`: Compiled registry JSON files per style
  - `registries.json`: Registry manifest

- **app/**: Next.js app directory
  - `(app)/`: Main documentation routes
  - `(view)/`: Component view routes
  - `(create)/`: Component creation tools
  - `api/`: API routes
  - `og/`: Open Graph image generation

**Config Files (v4):**
- `components.json`: shadcn/ui configuration (aliases, style, rsc enabled)
- `next.config.mjs`: Next.js configuration with turbopack
- `package.json`: v4-specific dependencies (Next.js 16, React 19, etc.)
- `postcss.config.mjs`: PostCSS/Tailwind config
- `source.config.ts`: Documentation config
- `tsconfig.scripts.json`: TypeScript config for build scripts

### packages/shadcn - Core CLI Package

The NPM package that users install. Structure:

- **src/index.ts**: CLI entry point
- **src/commands/**: CLI command implementations (add, init, etc.)
- **src/registry/**: Registry interface definitions
- **src/schema/**: Zod schemas for registry items and configurations
- **src/icons/**: Icon library definitions
- **src/styles/**: Style system definitions
- **src/templates/**: Project templates
- **src/preset/**: Tailwind preset configuration
- **src/utils/**: Utility functions
- **src/mcp/**: Model Context Protocol integration
- **src/migrations/**: Data migrations
- **src/preflights/**: Pre-flight checks
- **test/**: Vitest tests with fixtures

**Config:** Standard monorepo package with tsup build, exports for registry/schema/mcp/utils/icons/preset

### templates/ - Framework Templates

11 different starter templates:
- Next.js (app, monorepo)
- Vite (app, monorepo)
- React Router (app, monorepo)
- Astro (app, monorepo)
- Start (app, monorepo)

### Registry System

The project uses a sophisticated two-tier registry:

1. **File-based Registry** (apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/):
   - TypeScript files in organized directories
   - Generated registry exports via `_registry.ts` files in each category

2. **JSON Registry** (apps/v4/public/r/):
   - Compiled JSON output with component metadata
   - Built via `registry:build` script (uses `shadcn build` CLI + custom scripts)
   - Config presets with 12 theme combinations (6 style × 2 base: Radix/Base-UI)

### Component Structure Example

Components use:
- **CVA (Class Variance Authority)**: Variant management
- **Radix UI**: Unstyled primitives
- **Tailwind CSS**: Styling
- **TypeScript**: Type safety
- **React 19**: Latest React features

A typical component (e.g., button.tsx):
- Default export React component
- CVA-based variants
- Full TypeScript props
- Accessibility features
- Dark mode support via CSS variables

### Build & Development

**Root Scripts:**
- `turbo run build`: Build all packages
- `registry:build`: Build component registry (v4-specific)
- `dev`: Parallel development servers
- `lint`, `format:check`, `typecheck`: Code quality

**Package Manager:** pnpm 9.0.6

**Key Dependencies:** TypeScript 5.5, Tailwind CSS 4, Vite 7, Next.js 16, React 19, Radix UI 1.4.3, Lucide React 0.474.0

This is a professional, scalable UI component library designed for both internal use (v4 docs site) and distribution as an npm package for the wider community.

Perfect! Now let me compile the comprehensive report based on my findings. Let me create a summary document:

## Summary of Registry Structure and Demo Patterns

Based on my thorough exploration of the shadcn/ui codebase, here's a comprehensive understanding of how the component registry is structured and how demo/example files are organized:

### 1. REGISTRY SCHEMA & TYPE DEFINITIONS

**Location:** `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`

The registry uses Zod schemas to define strict type validation. Key types include:

```
Registry Item Types:
- "registry:lib"        (libraries/utilities)
- "registry:block"      (blocks/templates)
- "registry:component"  (composable components)
- "registry:ui"         (UI components)
- "registry:hook"       (React hooks)
- "registry:page"       (full pages)
- "registry:file"       (generic files)
- "registry:theme"      (theme configurations)
- "registry:style"      (style definitions)
- "registry:item"       (generic items)
- "registry:base"       (base configurations)
- "registry:font"       (font definitions)
- "registry:example"    (example/demo files - internal use)
- "registry:internal"   (internal utilities)
```

**Core Registry Item Schema** includes:
- `name` (required) - Unique identifier
- `title` (optional) - Display name
- `type` (required) - Registry item type enum
- `description` (optional) - Component description
- `files` (optional) - Array of file paths and metadata
- `dependencies` - npm dependencies
- `devDependencies` - dev-only dependencies
- `registryDependencies` - dependencies on other registry items
- `tailwind` - Tailwind configuration (theme, plugins, content)
- `cssVars` - CSS variable definitions (light/dark/theme)
- `css` - CSS property object
- `envVars` - Environment variables
- `meta` - Custom metadata object
- `docs` - Documentation URL
- `categories` - Component categories

### 2. REGISTRY FILE STRUCTURE

**Main Registry Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/registry.ts`

The main registry imports from multiple sub-registries:

```typescript
import { examples } from "./examples/_registry"
import { components } from "./components/_registry"
import { ui } from "./ui/_registry"
import { blocks } from "./blocks/_registry"
import { hooks } from "./hooks/_registry"
import { lib } from "./lib/_registry"
import { internal } from "./internal/_registry"
import { fonts } from "@/registry/fonts"
```

Then combines them into a single `Registry` object:
```typescript
export const registry = {
  name: "shadcn/ui",
  homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com",
  items: z.array(registryItemSchema).parse([
    { name: "index", ...RADIX_STYLE },
    { name: "style", ...RADIX_STYLE },
    ...ui,
    ...examples,
    ...lib,
    ...components,
    ...internal,
    ...blocks,
    ...hooks,
    ...fonts,
  ]),
} satisfies Registry
```

### 3. DEMO/EXAMPLE FILES STRUCTURE

**Examples Registry Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts`

Examples are registered as `type: "registry:example"` with the following pattern:

```typescript
{
  name: "button-example",           // Must end with "-example"
  title: "Button",                  // Display name
  type: "registry:example",         // Always this type
  registryDependencies: [           // Components this example uses
    "button", 
    "example"
  ],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/button-example.tsx",  // Relative path in examples folder
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

### 4. DEMO FILE PATTERNS (3 Examples)

**Example 1: Button Example**
**File:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/button-example.tsx`

Pattern:
- Default export function named descriptively (e.g., `ButtonExample`)
- Uses `ExampleWrapper` to wrap all examples (provides grid layout)
- Each subsection is a separate component function (e.g., `ButtonVariantsAndSizes`, `ButtonIconRight`)
- Uses `Example` component to wrap each subsection with a title
- Imports `Example` and `ExampleWrapper` from `@/registry/bases/radix/components/example`
- Imports actual UI components from `@/registry/bases/radix/ui/...`
- Shows all variants/states of the component

**Example 2: Accordion Example**
**File:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/accordion-example.tsx`

Pattern (similar to Button):
- Default export `AccordionExample` function
- Wrapped in `ExampleWrapper` with grid layout
- Multiple internal functions: `AccordionBasic`, `AccordionMultiple`, `AccordionWithBorders`, `AccordionInCard`, `AccordionWithDisabled`
- Each wrapped with `Example` component and a title
- Demonstrates different use cases and configurations
- Uses `Card`, `CardHeader`, etc. from UI registry

**Example 3: Alert Example**
**File:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/alert-example.tsx`

Pattern:
- Default export `AlertExample` function
- Wrapped in `ExampleWrapper`
- Subfunctions: `AlertExample1`, `AlertExample2`, `AlertExample3`, etc.
- Each shows different variant: "Basic", "With Icons", "Destructive", "With Actions", "Inside Card"
- Uses supporting components like `Card`, `Badge`, `Button`, `Input`, `Label`, `Select`
- Uses `IconPlaceholder` for dynamic icon rendering

### 5. HELPER COMPONENTS FOR EXAMPLES

**Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/components/example.tsx`

Two main wrapper components:

```typescript
// ExampleWrapper - Container for all examples (provides grid/layout)
function ExampleWrapper({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  // Grid layout: 2 columns on desktop, stacked on mobile
  // Min-height screen, max-width 5xl (2xl: 6xl)
  // Gap: 8px (sm: 12px, md: 8px)
}

// Example - Individual example section wrapper
function Example({
  title,        // Section title shown above content
  children,     // The example content
  className,
  containerClassName,
}) {
  // Blue-gray card background
  // Title in small font above
  // Content in card with padding
}
```

### 6. HOW COMPONENTS ARE EXPORTED/IMPORTED

**UI Component Registry Pattern:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts`

```typescript
export const ui: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "accordion",
    type: "registry:ui",
    files: [
      {
        path: "ui/accordion.tsx",
        type: "registry:ui",
      },
    ],
    meta: {
      links: {
        docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/accordion",
        examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/...",
        api: "https://www.radix-ui.com/...",
      },
    },
  },
  // ... more components
]
```

**Linking Examples to Components:**
- In the UI registry's `meta.links.examples`, the GitHub raw URL points to the example file
- The example name (e.g., "accordion-example") corresponds to component name + "-example"
- Example has `registryDependencies` that lists the components it uses

### 7. PATTERN FOR ADDING NEW COMPONENTS

To add a new component/demo:

1. **Create UI Component:** `ui/new-component.tsx`
2. **Add to UI Registry:** Add entry to `ui/_registry.ts`
3. **Create Example File:** `examples/new-component-example.tsx`
4. **Add to Examples Registry:** Add entry to `examples/_registry.ts` with:
   - `name: "new-component-example"`
   - `type: "registry:example"`
   - `registryDependencies: ["new-component", "example"]`
   - `files: [{ path: "examples/new-component-example.tsx", type: "registry:example" }]`
5. **Update Main Registry:** Both the UI and examples are auto-imported in `registry.ts`

### 8. KEY FILES REFERENCE

| Purpose | Path |
|---------|------|
| Registry schemas & types | `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts` |
| Registry API functions | `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/api.ts` |
| Main registry definition | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/registry.ts` |
| UI components registry | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` |
| Examples registry | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` |
| Example wrapper components | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/components/example.tsx` |
| Button example | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/button-example.tsx` |
| Accordion example | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/accordion-example.tsx` |
| Alert example | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/alert-example.tsx` |

This structure allows shadcn/ui to maintain a clean, scalable component library with comprehensive examples that are fully typed and registered for easy discovery and installation.

## Summary of Registry Structure and Demo Patterns

Based on my thorough exploration of the shadcn/ui codebase, here's a comprehensive understanding of how the component registry is structured and how demo/example files are organized:

### 1. REGISTRY SCHEMA & TYPE DEFINITIONS

**Location:** `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`

The registry uses Zod schemas to define strict type validation. Key types include:

```
Registry Item Types:
- "registry:lib"        (libraries/utilities)
- "registry:block"      (blocks/templates)
- "registry:component"  (composable components)
- "registry:ui"         (UI components)
- "registry:hook"       (React hooks)
- "registry:page"       (full pages)
- "registry:file"       (generic files)
- "registry:theme"      (theme configurations)
- "registry:style"      (style definitions)
- "registry:item"       (generic items)
- "registry:base"       (base configurations)
- "registry:font"       (font definitions)
- "registry:example"    (example/demo files - internal use)
- "registry:internal"   (internal utilities)
```

**Core Registry Item Schema** includes:
- `name` (required) - Unique identifier
- `title` (optional) - Display name
- `type` (required) - Registry item type enum
- `description` (optional) - Component description
- `files` (optional) - Array of file paths and metadata
- `dependencies` - npm dependencies
- `devDependencies` - dev-only dependencies
- `registryDependencies` - dependencies on other registry items
- `tailwind` - Tailwind configuration (theme, plugins, content)
- `cssVars` - CSS variable definitions (light/dark/theme)
- `css` - CSS property object
- `envVars` - Environment variables
- `meta` - Custom metadata object
- `docs` - Documentation URL
- `categories` - Component categories

### 2. REGISTRY FILE STRUCTURE

**Main Registry Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/registry.ts`

The main registry imports from multiple sub-registries:

```typescript
import { examples } from "./examples/_registry"
import { components } from "./components/_registry"
import { ui } from "./ui/_registry"
import { blocks } from "./blocks/_registry"
import { hooks } from "./hooks/_registry"
import { lib } from "./lib/_registry"
import { internal } from "./internal/_registry"
import { fonts } from "@/registry/fonts"
```

Then combines them into a single `Registry` object:
```typescript
export const registry = {
  name: "shadcn/ui",
  homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com",
  items: z.array(registryItemSchema).parse([
    { name: "index", ...RADIX_STYLE },
    { name: "style", ...RADIX_STYLE },
    ...ui,
    ...examples,
    ...lib,
    ...components,
    ...internal,
    ...blocks,
    ...hooks,
    ...fonts,
  ]),
} satisfies Registry
```

### 3. DEMO/EXAMPLE FILES STRUCTURE

**Examples Registry Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts`

Examples are registered as `type: "registry:example"` with the following pattern:

```typescript
{
  name: "button-example",           // Must end with "-example"
  title: "Button",                  // Display name
  type: "registry:example",         // Always this type
  registryDependencies: [           // Components this example uses
    "button", 
    "example"
  ],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/button-example.tsx",  // Relative path in examples folder
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

### 4. DEMO FILE PATTERNS (3 Examples)

**Example 1: Button Example**
**File:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/button-example.tsx`

Pattern:
- Default export function named descriptively (e.g., `ButtonExample`)
- Uses `ExampleWrapper` to wrap all examples (provides grid layout)
- Each subsection is a separate component function (e.g., `ButtonVariantsAndSizes`, `ButtonIconRight`)
- Uses `Example` component to wrap each subsection with a title
- Imports `Example` and `ExampleWrapper` from `@/registry/bases/radix/components/example`
- Imports actual UI components from `@/registry/bases/radix/ui/...`
- Shows all variants/states of the component

**Example 2: Accordion Example**
**File:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/accordion-example.tsx`

Pattern (similar to Button):
- Default export `AccordionExample` function
- Wrapped in `ExampleWrapper` with grid layout
- Multiple internal functions: `AccordionBasic`, `AccordionMultiple`, `AccordionWithBorders`, `AccordionInCard`, `AccordionWithDisabled`
- Each wrapped with `Example` component and a title
- Demonstrates different use cases and configurations
- Uses `Card`, `CardHeader`, etc. from UI registry

**Example 3: Alert Example**
**File:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/alert-example.tsx`

Pattern:
- Default export `AlertExample` function
- Wrapped in `ExampleWrapper`
- Subfunctions: `AlertExample1`, `AlertExample2`, `AlertExample3`, etc.
- Each shows different variant: "Basic", "With Icons", "Destructive", "With Actions", "Inside Card"
- Uses supporting components like `Card`, `Badge`, `Button`, `Input`, `Label`, `Select`
- Uses `IconPlaceholder` for dynamic icon rendering

### 5. HELPER COMPONENTS FOR EXAMPLES

**Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/components/example.tsx`

Two main wrapper components:

```typescript
// ExampleWrapper - Container for all examples (provides grid/layout)
function ExampleWrapper({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  // Grid layout: 2 columns on desktop, stacked on mobile
  // Min-height screen, max-width 5xl (2xl: 6xl)
  // Gap: 8px (sm: 12px, md: 8px)
}

// Example - Individual example section wrapper
function Example({
  title,        // Section title shown above content
  children,     // The example content
  className,
  containerClassName,
}) {
  // Blue-gray card background
  // Title in small font above
  // Content in card with padding
}
```

### 6. HOW COMPONENTS ARE EXPORTED/IMPORTED

**UI Component Registry Pattern:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts`

```typescript
export const ui: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "accordion",
    type: "registry:ui",
    files: [
      {
        path: "ui/accordion.tsx",
        type: "registry:ui",
      },
    ],
    meta: {
      links: {
        docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/accordion",
        examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/...",
        api: "https://www.radix-ui.com/...",
      },
    },
  },
  // ... more components
]
```

**Linking Examples to Components:**
- In the UI registry's `meta.links.examples`, the GitHub raw URL points to the example file
- The example name (e.g., "accordion-example") corresponds to component name + "-example"
- Example has `registryDependencies` that lists the components it uses

### 7. PATTERN FOR ADDING NEW COMPONENTS

To add a new component/demo:

1. **Create UI Component:** `ui/new-component.tsx`
2. **Add to UI Registry:** Add entry to `ui/_registry.ts`
3. **Create Example File:** `examples/new-component-example.tsx`
4. **Add to Examples Registry:** Add entry to `examples/_registry.ts` with:
   - `name: "new-component-example"`
   - `type: "registry:example"`
   - `registryDependencies: ["new-component", "example"]`
   - `files: [{ path: "examples/new-component-example.tsx", type: "registry:example" }]`
5. **Update Main Registry:** Both the UI and examples are auto-imported in `registry.ts`

### 8. KEY FILES REFERENCE

| Purpose | Path |
|---------|------|
| Registry schemas & types | `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts` |
| Registry API functions | `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/api.ts` |
| Main registry definition | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/registry.ts` |
| UI components registry | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` |
| Examples registry | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` |
| Example wrapper components | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/components/example.tsx` |
| Button example | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/button-example.tsx` |
| Accordion example | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/accordion-example.tsx` |
| Alert example | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/alert-example.tsx` |

This structure allows shadcn/ui to maintain a clean, scalable component library with comprehensive examples that are fully typed and registered for easy discovery and installation.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the compound component patterns in this codebase. Let me create a summary document of my findings.

## Compound Components in shadcn/ui - Analysis Report

Based on my exploration of the shadcn/ui codebase, I've identified clear patterns for how compound components are structured. Here's my detailed analysis:

---

### 1. **Compound Components Found**

The codebase contains several excellent examples of compound components:
- **Tabs** (tabs.tsx)
- **Accordion** (accordion.tsx)
- **Dialog** (dialog.tsx)
- **Popover** (popover.tsx)
- **Dropdown Menu** (dropdown-menu.tsx)
- **Select** (select.tsx)

---

### 2. **Core Structural Pattern**

All compound components follow a consistent pattern where:

**A. Base wrapper component:**
```
Tabs → TabsPrimitive.Root
Accordion → AccordionPrimitive.Root
Dialog → DialogPrimitive.Root
```

**B. Sub-components as named exports:**
```typescript
export { 
  Tabs, 
  TabsList, 
  TabsTrigger, 
  TabsContent, 
  tabsListVariants 
}
```

The components are **NOT** exported as nested properties (e.g., `Tabs.List`), but rather as **individual named exports** that are used together.

---

### 3. **State Management via React Context (Headless UI Libraries)**

These shadcn/ui components wrap underlying headless UI libraries that manage state:

**For Base UI components** (@base-ui/react):
- `Tabs` uses `@base-ui/react/tabs` 
- `Accordion` uses `@base-ui/react/accordion`
- `Dialog` uses `@base-ui/react/dialog`

**For Radix UI components** (radix-ui):
- Alternative implementations using `radix-ui` library

The **underlying primitives handle context** to share state between sub-components. The shadcn/ui wrapper components pass through the primitive's context automatically by extending their Props types.

---

### 4. **Export Pattern Examples**

**Tabs Component** (`/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/tabs.tsx`):
```typescript
export { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent, tabsListVariants }
```

**Accordion Component** (`/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/accordion.tsx`):
```typescript
export { Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent }
```

**Dialog Component** (`/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/dialog.tsx`):
```typescript
export {
  Dialog,
  DialogClose,
  DialogContent,
  DialogDescription,
  DialogFooter,
  DialogHeader,
  DialogOverlay,
  DialogPortal,
  DialogTitle,
  DialogTrigger,
}
```

**Dropdown Menu Component** (`/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx`):
```typescript
export {
  DropdownMenu,
  DropdownMenuPortal,
  DropdownMenuTrigger,
  DropdownMenuContent,
  DropdownMenuGroup,
  DropdownMenuLabel,
  DropdownMenuItem,
  DropdownMenuCheckboxItem,
  DropdownMenuRadioGroup,
  DropdownMenuRadioItem,
  DropdownMenuSeparator,
  DropdownMenuShortcut,
  DropdownMenuSub,
  DropdownMenuSubTrigger,
  DropdownMenuSubContent,
}
```

---

### 5. **TypeScript Type Definitions Pattern**

Component Props are typed in **two ways**:

**For Base UI:**
```typescript
function Tabs({
  className,
  orientation = "horizontal",
  ...props
}: TabsPrimitive.Root.Props) {
  return <TabsPrimitive.Root ... {...props} />
}
```

**For Radix UI:**
```typescript
function Tabs({
  className,
  orientation = "horizontal",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.Root>) {
  return <TabsPrimitive.Root ... {...props} />
}
```

**Key patterns:**
- Props types are **spread from the underlying primitive** (`TabsPrimitive.Root.Props` or `React.ComponentProps<typeof ...>`)
- Custom props are **explicitly defined** (e.g., `orientation`, `variant`, `showCloseButton`)
- Props extend with `&` for merged types: `SelectPrimitive.Popup.Props & VariantProps<typeof tabsListVariants>`

---

### 6. **Orientation Support Pattern (Horizontal/Vertical)**

Orientation is consistently handled through:

**1. Default parameter:**
```typescript
function Tabs({
  className,
  orientation = "horizontal",
  ...props
}: TabsPrimitive.Root.Props) {
```

**2. Data attribute set on root:**
```typescript
<TabsPrimitive.Root
  data-orientation={orientation}
  className={cn(
    "cn-tabs group/tabs flex data-horizontal:flex-col",
    className
  )}
  {...props}
/>
```

**3. Child components respect via Tailwind modifiers:**
```typescript
className={cn(
  "... group-data-vertical/tabs:h-fit group-data-vertical/tabs:flex-col",
  className
)}
```

**Usage example:**
```typescript
<Tabs defaultValue="account" orientation="vertical">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="account">Account</TabsTrigger>
    ...
  </TabsList>
</Tabs>
```

---

### 7. **Consistent Naming & Slot Conventions**

**Data slot attributes** are used for styling hooks:
```typescript
<TabsPrimitive.Root data-slot="tabs" ... />
<TabsPrimitive.List data-slot="tabs-list" ... />
<TabsPrimitive.Tab data-slot="tabs-trigger" ... />
<TabsPrimitive.Panel data-slot="tabs-content" ... />
```

**CSS class naming pattern:**
```
cn-{component}-{subpart}
cn-tabs
cn-tabs-list
cn-tabs-trigger
cn-tabs-content
```

---

### 8. **Key Differences Between Base UI & Radix Implementations**

Both follow the same shadcn/ui pattern, but differ in underlying APIs:

**Base UI specifics:**
- Uses custom prop interfaces: `TabsPrimitive.Root.Props`
- Wraps with `render` prop: `<Button render={<IconPlaceholder ... />} />`
- Custom positioning: `Positioner`, `Portal` components

**Radix UI specifics:**
- Uses standard React component props: `React.ComponentProps<typeof ...>`
- Uses `asChild` prop for composition: `<Close asChild><Button>...</Button></Close>`
- Built-in positioning via Content component props

---

### 9. **Composition Techniques**

**Portal Management:**
```typescript
function DialogContent({...}) {
  return (
    <DialogPortal>
      <DialogOverlay />
      <DialogPrimitive.Popup {...props}>
        {children}
        {showCloseButton && <DialogPrimitive.Close ... />}
      </DialogPrimitive.Popup>
    </DialogPortal>
  )
}
```

**Nested Compound Components:**
```typescript
// DropdownMenu supports nested submenus
<DropdownMenuSub>
  <DropdownMenuSubTrigger>...</DropdownMenuSubTrigger>
  <DropdownMenuSubContent>
    {/* nested content */}
  </DropdownMenuSubContent>
</DropdownMenuSub>
```

---

### 10. **File Locations**

- **Base implementations:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/`
- **Radix implementations:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/`
- **Styled versions:** `/apps/v4/styles/{style-name}/ui/`
- **Examples:** `/apps/v4/examples/base/` and `/apps/v4/examples/radix/`

---

### Summary of Key Takeaways

1. **Named exports pattern** (not nested dot notation) - all sub-components exported separately
2. **Wrapper functions** that extend primitive types and apply consistent styling (cn-prefixed classes)
3. **Context from headless UI libraries** - primitives handle state sharing automatically
4. **Data attributes** for styling hooks and styling variations
5. **Orientation support** via default parameters and data attributes + Tailwind group selectors
6. **Type safety** through extending primitive prop types
7. **Consistent naming conventions** with `cn-` prefix and semantic naming
8. **Composition over inheritance** - Portal, Overlay, and Content components wrapped separately
9. **Variant support** using CVA (Class Variance Authority)
10. **Multiple UI library support** - Same shadcn/ui API wraps both Base UI and Radix UI primitives

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the compound component patterns in this codebase. Let me create a summary document of my findings.

## Compound Components in shadcn/ui - Analysis Report

Based on my exploration of the shadcn/ui codebase, I've identified clear patterns for how compound components are structured. Here's my detailed analysis:

---

### 1. **Compound Components Found**

The codebase contains several excellent examples of compound components:
- **Tabs** (tabs.tsx)
- **Accordion** (accordion.tsx)
- **Dialog** (dialog.tsx)
- **Popover** (popover.tsx)
- **Dropdown Menu** (dropdown-menu.tsx)
- **Select** (select.tsx)

---

### 2. **Core Structural Pattern**

All compound components follow a consistent pattern where:

**A. Base wrapper component:**
```
Tabs → TabsPrimitive.Root
Accordion → AccordionPrimitive.Root
Dialog → DialogPrimitive.Root
```

**B. Sub-components as named exports:**
```typescript
export { 
  Tabs, 
  TabsList, 
  TabsTrigger, 
  TabsContent, 
  tabsListVariants 
}
```

The components are **NOT** exported as nested properties (e.g., `Tabs.List`), but rather as **individual named exports** that are used together.

---

### 3. **State Management via React Context (Headless UI Libraries)**

These shadcn/ui components wrap underlying headless UI libraries that manage state:

**For Base UI components** (@base-ui/react):
- `Tabs` uses `@base-ui/react/tabs` 
- `Accordion` uses `@base-ui/react/accordion`
- `Dialog` uses `@base-ui/react/dialog`

**For Radix UI components** (radix-ui):
- Alternative implementations using `radix-ui` library

The **underlying primitives handle context** to share state between sub-components. The shadcn/ui wrapper components pass through the primitive's context automatically by extending their Props types.

---

### 4. **Export Pattern Examples**

**Tabs Component** (`/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/tabs.tsx`):
```typescript
export { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent, tabsListVariants }
```

**Accordion Component** (`/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/accordion.tsx`):
```typescript
export { Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent }
```

**Dialog Component** (`/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/dialog.tsx`):
```typescript
export {
  Dialog,
  DialogClose,
  DialogContent,
  DialogDescription,
  DialogFooter,
  DialogHeader,
  DialogOverlay,
  DialogPortal,
  DialogTitle,
  DialogTrigger,
}
```

**Dropdown Menu Component** (`/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx`):
```typescript
export {
  DropdownMenu,
  DropdownMenuPortal,
  DropdownMenuTrigger,
  DropdownMenuContent,
  DropdownMenuGroup,
  DropdownMenuLabel,
  DropdownMenuItem,
  DropdownMenuCheckboxItem,
  DropdownMenuRadioGroup,
  DropdownMenuRadioItem,
  DropdownMenuSeparator,
  DropdownMenuShortcut,
  DropdownMenuSub,
  DropdownMenuSubTrigger,
  DropdownMenuSubContent,
}
```

---

### 5. **TypeScript Type Definitions Pattern**

Component Props are typed in **two ways**:

**For Base UI:**
```typescript
function Tabs({
  className,
  orientation = "horizontal",
  ...props
}: TabsPrimitive.Root.Props) {
  return <TabsPrimitive.Root ... {...props} />
}
```

**For Radix UI:**
```typescript
function Tabs({
  className,
  orientation = "horizontal",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.Root>) {
  return <TabsPrimitive.Root ... {...props} />
}
```

**Key patterns:**
- Props types are **spread from the underlying primitive** (`TabsPrimitive.Root.Props` or `React.ComponentProps<typeof ...>`)
- Custom props are **explicitly defined** (e.g., `orientation`, `variant`, `showCloseButton`)
- Props extend with `&` for merged types: `SelectPrimitive.Popup.Props & VariantProps<typeof tabsListVariants>`

---

### 6. **Orientation Support Pattern (Horizontal/Vertical)**

Orientation is consistently handled through:

**1. Default parameter:**
```typescript
function Tabs({
  className,
  orientation = "horizontal",
  ...props
}: TabsPrimitive.Root.Props) {
```

**2. Data attribute set on root:**
```typescript
<TabsPrimitive.Root
  data-orientation={orientation}
  className={cn(
    "cn-tabs group/tabs flex data-horizontal:flex-col",
    className
  )}
  {...props}
/>
```

**3. Child components respect via Tailwind modifiers:**
```typescript
className={cn(
  "... group-data-vertical/tabs:h-fit group-data-vertical/tabs:flex-col",
  className
)}
```

**Usage example:**
```typescript
<Tabs defaultValue="account" orientation="vertical">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="account">Account</TabsTrigger>
    ...
  </TabsList>
</Tabs>
```

---

### 7. **Consistent Naming & Slot Conventions**

**Data slot attributes** are used for styling hooks:
```typescript
<TabsPrimitive.Root data-slot="tabs" ... />
<TabsPrimitive.List data-slot="tabs-list" ... />
<TabsPrimitive.Tab data-slot="tabs-trigger" ... />
<TabsPrimitive.Panel data-slot="tabs-content" ... />
```

**CSS class naming pattern:**
```
cn-{component}-{subpart}
cn-tabs
cn-tabs-list
cn-tabs-trigger
cn-tabs-content
```

---

### 8. **Key Differences Between Base UI & Radix Implementations**

Both follow the same shadcn/ui pattern, but differ in underlying APIs:

**Base UI specifics:**
- Uses custom prop interfaces: `TabsPrimitive.Root.Props`
- Wraps with `render` prop: `<Button render={<IconPlaceholder ... />} />`
- Custom positioning: `Positioner`, `Portal` components

**Radix UI specifics:**
- Uses standard React component props: `React.ComponentProps<typeof ...>`
- Uses `asChild` prop for composition: `<Close asChild><Button>...</Button></Close>`
- Built-in positioning via Content component props

---

### 9. **Composition Techniques**

**Portal Management:**
```typescript
function DialogContent({...}) {
  return (
    <DialogPortal>
      <DialogOverlay />
      <DialogPrimitive.Popup {...props}>
        {children}
        {showCloseButton && <DialogPrimitive.Close ... />}
      </DialogPrimitive.Popup>
    </DialogPortal>
  )
}
```

**Nested Compound Components:**
```typescript
// DropdownMenu supports nested submenus
<DropdownMenuSub>
  <DropdownMenuSubTrigger>...</DropdownMenuSubTrigger>
  <DropdownMenuSubContent>
    {/* nested content */}
  </DropdownMenuSubContent>
</DropdownMenuSub>
```

---

### 10. **File Locations**

- **Base implementations:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/`
- **Radix implementations:** `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/`
- **Styled versions:** `/apps/v4/styles/{style-name}/ui/`
- **Examples:** `/apps/v4/examples/base/` and `/apps/v4/examples/radix/`

---

### Summary of Key Takeaways

1. **Named exports pattern** (not nested dot notation) - all sub-components exported separately
2. **Wrapper functions** that extend primitive types and apply consistent styling (cn-prefixed classes)
3. **Context from headless UI libraries** - primitives handle state sharing automatically
4. **Data attributes** for styling hooks and styling variations
5. **Orientation support** via default parameters and data attributes + Tailwind group selectors
6. **Type safety** through extending primitive prop types
7. **Consistent naming conventions** with `cn-` prefix and semantic naming
8. **Composition over inheritance** - Portal, Overlay, and Content components wrapped separately
9. **Variant support** using CVA (Class Variance Authority)
10. **Multiple UI library support** - Same shadcn/ui API wraps both Base UI and Radix UI primitives

content.thinking: Now I have a very thorough understanding of the project. Let me also quickly check if there's a `separator.tsx` in the UI directory (since the stepper needs a separator), as well as check the button.tsx to understand how simple components are written.

content.text: Now let me check a few more things — the separator component (since Stepper needs it) and look at a component that uses custom React context (not a Radix primitive) for reference:

1	"use client"
2	
3	import * as React from "react"
4	import { Separator as SeparatorPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
5	
6	import { cn } from "@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils"
7	
8	function Separator({
9	  className,
10	  orientation = "horizontal",
11	  decorative = true,
12	  ...props
13	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof SeparatorPrimitive.Root>) {
14	  return (
15	    <SeparatorPrimitive.Root
16	      data-slot="separator"
17	      decorative={decorative}
18	      orientation={orientation}
19	      className={cn(
20	        "shrink-0 bg-border data-horizontal:h-px data-horizontal:w-full data-vertical:w-px data-vertical:self-stretch",
21	        className
22	      )}
23	      {...props}
24	    />
25	  )
26	}
27	
28	export { Separator }
29

input.content:
# Plan: Add Stepper Compound Component ## Context The project needs a `Stepper` UI component for building multi-step wizard and onboarding flows. It does not exist in the library today. The Stepper must follow all established shadcn/ui v4 patterns: `"use client"`, `cn()` utility, `data-slot` attributes, `cn-` CSS class prefixes, group-based orientation styling via Tailwind data-attribute selectors, and named exports. Since there is no suitable Radix UI primitive for a stepper, we implement the context layer ourselves (same approach as `sidebar.tsx`). **Constraint:** Do NOT modify any existing component files. Only create new files and append registry entries. --- ## Files to Create 1. `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` 2. `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx` ## Files to Modify (append-only additions) 3. `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` — add `stepper` entry (alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`) 4. `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` — add `stepper-example` entry (alphabetically between `spinner-example` and `switch-example`) --- ## Step 1 — Create `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` ### Design decisions - **No external dependencies.** Pure React + Tailwind + the existing `cn()` utility. - **Two context layers:** - `StepperContext` — root-level: active `value`, `onValueChange`, `orientation`, `steps` order array - `StepperItemContext` — per-item: `value`, `isActive`, `isCompleted`, `isPending` - **Controlled + uncontrolled:** `value`/`onValueChange` for controlled, `defaultValue` for uncontrolled (defaults to `steps[0]`). - **Orientation:** `"horizontal"` (default) | `"vertical"`. Set as `data-orientation` on root; children use `group-data-horizontal/stepper:` and `group-data-vertical/stepper:` Tailwind modifiers. - **Step ordering / completion:** `steps` string array passed to the root defines render order; each `StepperItem` computes `isCompleted` via `steps.indexOf(itemValue) < steps.indexOf(activeValue)`. - **State expressed as data attributes:** `data-active`, `data-completed`, `data-pending` — enables style overrides from the style system. - **`StepperPanel`:** accepts a `value` prop; renders children only when `value === activeValue` (unmounts otherwise — appropriate for wizard flows). - **`StepperSeparator`:** reads orientation from context; rendered as a sibling between `StepperItem` elements. - **Accessibility:** `StepperTrigger` sets `aria-current="step"` when active; `StepperSeparator` is `aria-hidden`. ### Sub-components & their props | Component | Base element | Key props | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | `Stepper` | `
` | `value`, `defaultValue`, `onValueChange`, `orientation`, `steps`, + all div props | Root, provides both contexts | | `StepperItem` | `
` | `value` (required), + all div props | Provides item context | | `StepperTrigger` | `

Since there is no suitable Radix UI primitive for a stepper, we implement the context layer ourselves (same approach as `sidebar.tsx`). **Constraint:** Do NOT modify any existing component files. Only create new files and append registry entries. --- ## Files to Create 1. `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` 2. `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx` ## Files to Modify (append-only additions) 3. `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` — add `stepper` entry (alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`) 4. `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` — add `stepper-example` entry (alphabetically between `spinner-example` and `switch-example`) --- ## Step 1 — Create `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` ### Design decisions - **No external dependencies.** Pure React + Tailwind + the existing `cn()` utility. - **Two context layers:** - `StepperContext` — root-level: active `value`, `onValueChange`, `orientation`, `steps` order array - `StepperItemContext` — per-item: `value`, `isActive`, `isCompleted`, `isPending` - **Controlled + uncontrolled:** `value`/`onValueChange` for controlled, `defaultValue` for uncontrolled (defaults to `steps[0]`). - **Orientation:** `"horizontal"` (default) | `"vertical"`. Set as `data-orientation` on root; children use `group-data-horizontal/stepper:` and `group-data-vertical/stepper:` Tailwind modifiers. - **Step ordering / completion:** `steps` string array passed to the root defines render order; each `StepperItem` computes `isCompleted` via `steps.indexOf(itemValue) < steps.indexOf(activeValue)`. - **State expressed as data attributes:** `data-active`, `data-completed`, `data-pending` — enables style overrides from the style system. - **`StepperPanel`:** accepts a `value` prop; renders children only when `value === activeValue` (unmounts otherwise — appropriate for wizard flows). - **`StepperSeparator`:** reads orientation from context; rendered as a sibling between `StepperItem` elements. - **Accessibility:** `StepperTrigger` sets `aria-current="step"` when active; `StepperSeparator` is `aria-hidden`. ### Sub-components & their props | Component | Base element | Key props | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | `Stepper` | `
` | `value`, `defaultValue`, `onValueChange`, `orientation`, `steps`, + all div props | Root, provides both contexts | | `StepperItem` | `
` | `value` (required), + all div props | Provides item context | | `StepperTrigger` | `

content.text:
Here is the complete implementation plan:

---

## Plan: Add Stepper Compound Component

**4 file operations total — 2 new files created, 2 registry entries inserted (no existing component files modified).**

---

### New file 1: `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx`

This is a fully custom compound component (no Radix primitive exists for steppers). It follows the `sidebar.tsx` pattern for custom React context.

**Two context layers:**
- **`StepperContext`** (root-level) — active `value`, `onValueChange`, `orientation`, `steps[]` (ordered string array for computing completion)
- **`StepperItemContext`** (per-item) — `value`, `isActive`, `isCompleted`, `isPending`

**Key design decisions:**
- Controlled (`value`/`onValueChange`) + uncontrolled (`defaultValue`) modes, defaults to `steps[0]`
- Orientation via `data-orientation` on root + `group/stepper` class, children use `group-data-horizontal/stepper:` / `group-data-vertical/stepper:` Tailwind selectors — identical to `tabs.tsx` pattern
- `isCompleted` is auto-computed via `steps.indexOf(itemValue) < steps.indexOf(activeValue)` — user just passes the `steps` array to the root
- State expressed as `data-active` / `data-completed` / `data-pending` on each `StepperItem` and `StepperTrigger` for the style system
- `StepperPanel value="step-id"` unmounts when inactive (fits wizard flows)
- `StepperSeparator` reads orientation from context, renders as a sibling between items
- `StepperTrigger` sets `aria-current="step"` when active; `StepperSeparator` is `aria-hidden`
- No npm dependencies — pure React + Tailwind + existing `cn()` utility

**Exports:** `Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperPanel`, `useStepper`

---

### New file 2: `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx`

Four demo sections following the exact `accordion-example.tsx` pattern (`ExampleWrapper` → `Example` → inner functions):

| Function | Example Title | What it shows |
|---|---|---|
| `StepperBasic` | "Basic" | Horizontal 3-step stepper with `useState` |
| `StepperVertical` | "Vertical" | Same steps, `orientation="vertical"` |
| `StepperWithPanels` | "With Content Panels" | Full wizard: step header + `StepperPanel` per step + Back/Next `Button` nav |
| `StepperInCard` | "In Card" | Horizontal stepper inside a `Card` |

---

### Registry insertion 1: `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts`

Add a `"stepper"` entry alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`. No `dependencies` or `registryDependencies` (self-contained component).

### Registry insertion 2: `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts`

Add a `"stepper-example"` entry alphabetically between `spinner-example` and `switch-example`, with `registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button", "card", "example"]`.

---

### Verification steps
1. `pnpm typecheck` — zero errors on new files
2. `pnpm dev` in `apps/v4/` — render the example visually
3. Check `data-active/completed/pending` attributes toggle on click
4. Confirm `StepperPanel` swaps correctly in the wizard demo
5. `pnpm registry:build` — both `stepper` and `stepper-example` appear in `public/r/`

Now let me provide you with a comprehensive summary of all the requested files and directory listings.

## Summary of Explored Files and Directories

### 1. sidebar.tsx (Full Content)
Path: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775473905955-omjl0g/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/sidebar.tsx`

This is a comprehensive 709-line UI component file with multiple exported components:
- `SidebarProvider` - Context provider managing sidebar state
- `Sidebar` - Main container with variants (sidebar/floating/inset) and collapsible options
- `SidebarTrigger` - Button to toggle sidebar
- `SidebarRail` - Drag-to-resize rail
- `SidebarInset` - Main content wrapper
- `SidebarInput`, `SidebarHeader`, `SidebarFooter`, `SidebarSeparator` - Layout components
- `SidebarContent`, `SidebarGroup`, `SidebarGroupLabel`, `SidebarGroupAction`, `SidebarGroupContent` - Grouping components

This includes secure password hashing and storage, multi-factor authentication (MFA) implementation, session management, OAuth2 and SSO integration, regular security audits, rate limiting to prevent brute force attacks, and maintaining detailed audit logs. Additionally, you'll need to consider scalability, performance impact, and compliance with relevant data protection regulations such as GDPR or HIPAA.", }, { value: "item-2", trigger: "How does modern distributed system architecture handle eventual consistency and data synchronization across multiple regions?", content: "Modern distributed systems employ various strategies to maintain data consistency across regions. This often involves using techniques like CRDT (Conflict-Free Replicated Data Types), vector clocks, and gossip protocols. Systems might implement event sourcing patterns, utilize message queues for asynchronous updates, and employ sophisticated conflict resolution strategies. Building out the separator and panel components...

Now I'm reconsidering how the StepperItem should be structured in horizontal orientation—the trigger needs to sit at the top of the stepper track, with content flowing below it.

Looking at typical stepper patterns, the layout really depends on orientation: horizontally, items flow in a row with triggers connected by horizontal separators, while vertically they stack with content offset to the right of the track line. The separator is the tricky part—whether it's embedded in each item or exists as its own element between them. Since `StepperSeparator` reads from context, I should make sure the orientation logic flows through properly.

The separator sits as a sibling between items, so in horizontal mode it becomes a thin horizontal line that stretches, and in vertical mode it becomes a vertical line. For `StepperItem` itself, the horizontal layout needs the container as a flex row with each item as flex-col...

Actually, I'm reconsidering the structure. The trigger probably just contains the circle indicator, while `StepperTitle` and `StepperDescription` are separate child components within the item. That way the user can compose them however they want, and the styling handles whether they stack vertically or sit alongside the trigger. I'm realizing the horizontal layout is trickier than I thought—the separators need to sit at the same vertical level as the circles while the titles appear below. The challenge is positioning those connecting lines cleanly without making the markup overly complex, so I need to think through whether the separator should be a sibling element or handled differently.

Looking at the component structure, the `StepperTrigger` is the clickable element that contains the visual circle indicator, while `StepperTitle` and `StepperDescription` are separate pieces for the text content. This means I need to figure out how these pieces fit together in the flex layout and how the separator aligns with the trigger's circle.

Building out the HTML structure... I'm realizing the components should stay flexible and let users compose them however they want, rather than enforcing a specific layout structure. The stepper container handles the overall direction (horizontal or vertical), and then each item and separator gets placed inside naturally—I don't need to dictate exactly how the trigger, title, and description nest together. The separator between items needs proper spacing to align with those circles, which is handled by the margin-top utility in horizontal mode.

4	
5	import { cn } from "@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils"
6	
7	const buttonVariants = cva(
8	  "cn-button group/button inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center whitespace-nowrap transition-all outline-none select-none disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0",
9	  {
10	    variants: {
11	      variant: {
12	        default: "cn-button-variant-default",
13	        outline: "cn-button-variant-outline",
14	        secondary: "cn-button-variant-secondary",
15	        ghost: "cn-button-variant-ghost",
16	        destructive: "cn-button-variant-destructive",
17	        link: "cn-button-variant-link",
18	      },
19	      size: {
20	        default: "cn-button-size-default",
21	        xs: "cn-button-size-xs",
22	        sm: "cn-button-size-sm",
23	        lg: "cn-button-size-lg",
24	        icon: "cn-button-size-icon",
25	        "icon-xs": "cn-button-size-icon-xs",
26	        "icon-sm": "cn-button-size-icon-sm",
27	        "icon-lg": "cn-button-size-icon-lg",
28	      },
29	    },
30	    defaultVariants: {
31	      variant: "default",
32	      size: "default",
33	    },
34	  }
35	)
36	
37	function Button({
38	  className,
39	  variant = "default",
40	  size = "default",
41	  asChild = false,
42	  ...props
43	}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
44	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
45	    asChild?: boolean
46	  }) {
47	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
48	
49	  return (
50	    <Comp
51	      data-slot="button"
52	      data-variant={variant}
53	      data-size={size}
54	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
55	      {...props}
56	    />
57	  )
58	}
59	
60	export { Button, buttonVariants }

The tailwind lib.js is compiled/minified JavaScript. Let me try a different approach - search for how `data-horizontal` maps to `data-orientation`.

Actually, let me just search for this in the Tailwind CSS documentation or look for the generated CSS output.

Actually, I think the most pragmatic approach at this point is to simply follow the exact patterns used in the existing components. If `separator.tsx` uses `data-horizontal:h-px` and it works with `data-orientation="horizontal"`, then I should use the same pattern.

Based on what I see in the existing code:


- `separator.tsx` uses Radix's `orientation` prop to set `data-orientation` and applies `data-horizontal:` / `data-vertical:` Tailwind classes
- `toggle-group.tsx` explicitly sets `data-orientation={orientation}` and uses `data-vertical:flex-col` on itself with `group-data-horizontal/toggle-group:` on children
- `tabs.tsx` applies `data-horizontal:flex-col` on the root and `group-data-vertical/tabs:flex-col` on children

It looks like Tailwind v4 treats `data-horizontal:` as matching `data-orientation="horizontal"`, so I should follow this same pattern. So the parent element has `data-orientation="horizontal"` or `data-orientation="vertical"`, and Tailwind v4 is matching these specific data attributes with selectors like `data-horizontal:` and `data-vertical:`. The child elements then use `group-data-horizontal/toggle-group:` to target styles based on the parent's data attribute, which means Tailwind is generating selectors that match `[data-orientation="horizontal"]` when you write `data-horizontal:` in the class name. I'll follow the same pattern that's already working throughout the codebase—setting `data-orientation` on the root element and using `data-horizontal:` and `data-vertical:` Tailwind classes, along with `group/stepper` for child elements. Since this approach is proven in production, I don't need to overthink the CSS mechanics. Now I'm building out the component files, starting with the root `Stepper` element that uses flexbox with orientation-aware layout classes.

For `StepperItem`, I need to handle the different visual arrangements—horizontal steppers stack the circle and label vertically with centered alignment, while vertical steppers arrange them horizontally with the circle on the left. I'm using group selectors to apply these layout rules based on the parent's orientation. Looking at the actual visual structure, I'm realizing the separator line positioning needs careful consideration in the layout. I need to figure out the right spacing for the separator in horizontal mode—it should align with the center of the step circles, but since I don't know the exact trigger height, I'll keep it flexible and let users adjust via className. Now I'm thinking through the core structure: the root Stepper uses flexbox with a data attribute to switch between horizontal and vertical layouts, while StepperItem handles the column/row arrangement depending on orientation, with proper gap and alignment for each direction. I'm considering the structure for `StepperTrigger` and whether the title and description should live inside it or as separate siblings, weighing the flexibility of each approach.

Looking at how horizontal and vertical steppers typically render, I need to design the component structure to support both layouts cleanly—the trigger as the clickable circle or number, with the title and description positioned relative to it depending on orientation.

Building out the component structure... I'm thinking through how to handle the separator alignment in horizontal mode—it needs to stretch across and center vertically, but the exact positioning depends on whether we have labels below the step indicators. Using `self-center` on the separator itself might be the simplest approach rather than trying to calculate precise margins.

For the component structure, `StepperItem` and `StepperSeparator` are siblings under the root `Stepper`, so I need to think about how the flex layout distributes them—each item gets its own flex container for vertical stacking, while separators stretch between them. I'm settling on using `self-center` for the separator without hard-coded margins, since that should naturally align with the step indicator if the trigger buttons are consistent heights.

Writing component structure... Writing component structure...

Actually, I realize the separator should leverage the parent stepper's group selectors for orientation instead of reading it from context—that's cleaner and avoids redundant data attributes. I'm settling on reading orientation from context and setting `data-orientation` manually on the separator element, then using the same `data-horizontal:` and `data-vertical:` Tailwind pattern that Radix uses.

Now I'm writing out the component code... I'm setting up the internal state management for the Stepper component, using a controlled or uncontrolled pattern depending on whether a value prop is provided. Then I'm wrapping everything in a context provider so child components can access the current step, handle value changes, and know the orientation and available steps.

Now I'm building the StepperItem component, which takes a value prop and determines its state relative to the active step—whether it's the current active step, already completed, or still pending. I'm memoizing the item context with these computed states so child components can render accordingly.

Finishing the component structure... Writing component styles... Finishing up the stepper component structure...

Now I'm planning out four example variations to showcase the component: a basic horizontal stepper with clickable steps, a vertical orientation version, a full wizard-style example with panels and navigation buttons, and one integrated into a card layout. I'm starting to write the demo file that imports the necessary dependencies and sets up these different use cases.

Writing example components... Building out the remaining stepper items...

I'm realizing the data attributes like `data-active:` and `data-completed:` need to be applied to the elements that actually have those states—so both the `StepperItem` and `StepperTrigger` components need these conditional styles, not just the visual elements inside them.

This isn't getting me anywhere productive. Let me try a different approach - just look at how `data-horizontal:` actually works in the compiled CSS. OR I can approach this more practically: 

Since the existing codebase uses `data-horizontal:` and `data-vertical:` for elements that have `data-orientation="horizontal"` and `data-orientation="vertical"` respectively, and this codebase WORKS in production, I should just follow the same pattern.

The key insight from Tailwind v4 is that `data-horizontal:class` generates the CSS selector `[data-horizontal] { class }`. 

Wait, but the elements have `data-orientation="horizontal"`, not `data-horizontal`. Maybe Tailwind v4 has special handling where `data-horizontal` generates `[data-orientation=horizontal]` as the selector, or perhaps `data-[value]` syntax matches any data attribute containing that value. Let me check the Tailwind v4 documentation to understand how this actually works. So either Tailwind v4 is matching `data-horizontal:` against a `data-orientation="horizontal"` attribute, or these components are actually rendering both `data-orientation` and `data-horizontal` attributes simultaneously. I need to check the Radix UI source to see which one is actually happening.

From the Tailwind CSS v4 docs on data variants:

> **Data attributes**


> Use the `data-*` variant to conditionally style things based on data attributes.
> `data-size-large:text-lg` → `[data-size~="large"]`
> `data-disabled:opacity-50` → `[data-disabled]`

So the pattern is clear now — `data-disabled:opacity-50` matches the presence of a `data-disabled` attribute, while `data-size-large:text-lg` matches a specific value. Let me fix this. The issue is that I'm trying to use render props pattern for the trigger, but the component doesn't support that. I should use the `data-active` and `data-[completed]` attributes instead, OR use the `useStepper` / `useStepperItem` hooks (but we need to export `useStepperItem` for that).


Actually, the cleanest approach without exporting additional hooks is to manage the step state locally in the example and use that to conditionally apply styles to the children. Let me rewrite the example to track the current step and determine the styling based on that local state.

Building the example component... Writing the stepper component... I see the issue now—putting panels inside the `Stepper` component would break the layout since they'd get mixed into the flex row with the step items. The real solution is to restructure the example so the stepper acts as a proper context provider with the navigation at the top and content below, or explicitly control the flex direction to stack them vertically for a wizard-style layout.

But I'm realizing there's a naming conflict in my example: I'm importing `StepperPanel` from the component library at the top, then defining a local `StepperPanel` function at the bottom that shadows the import. That's going to cause problems.

I think the cleaner approach is to skip the import entirely and just use conditional rendering directly in the example with simple divs, or restructure things so `StepperPanel` lives inside the `<Stepper>` component and I adjust the layout to use flexbox columns instead. The second option is better because it actually demonstrates the `StepperPanel` component as intended.

Restructuring the wizard layout...

I think the cleanest approach is to keep the step navigation separate from the content panels—the header shows the steps in a row, and the panels render conditionally below based on the current step. This is how most real-world forms work anyway, so I'll skip demonstrating `StepperPanel` in the example and just use simple conditional rendering instead.

Actually, I could show `StepperPanel` by wrapping the whole thing in a `Stepper` with a flex-column override to create a vertical wizard layout. But then I'd need `StepperItem` and `StepperSeparator` as direct children for the flex to work properly—wrapping them in another div breaks that relationship. Though really, the `Stepper` is just a context provider, so the visual layout is entirely flexible. I can structure the wizard however I want. Writing component examples... I can use the `data-active` and `data-completed` attributes on the `StepperTrigger` to style child elements with group selectors, so I'd need to add the `group` class to the trigger and then use `group-data-[active]` and `group-data-[completed]` on the span to apply the right styles based on the step state.

Actually, the simpler approach is to compute the active and completed states directly from the current `step` value and the `STEPS` array—checking if the step matches the current one and whether we've already passed it in the sequence. I also notice there's a duplicate `StepperPanel` definition that I need to clean up, so let me reorganize the example to remove the import conflict and use local state properly.

content.text: Let me verify the `cn` utility handles class overrides correctly:

content.thinking:
`cn` uses `tailwind-merge` which intelligently handles Tailwind class conflicts. So when we have `flex data-vertical:flex-col` (base classes) and the user passes `flex-col` in `className`, `tailwind-merge` should correctly:
1. Keep `flex` (sets display: flex)
2. Override the implicit row direction with explicit `flex-col` (which has higher specificity because it's later in the string)

Actually wait - `tailwind-merge` handles this correctly. Since `flex-col` overrides any default flex direction, the merged result should output both `flex` and `flex-col` effectively. 

Since the user's `className` comes last in `cn(base, className)`, `tailwind-merge` will prefer `flex-col` over any implicit flex-direction from the base classes, which should work correctly. In `StepperWithPanels`, the combined classes will keep `flex` for the display property while using the user's `flex-col` for the direction override.

But I'm realizing `data-vertical:flex-col` is a conditional variant, not a regular class, so `tailwind-merge` might not recognize it as conflicting with the unconditional `flex-col` the user provides. Actually, `tailwind-merge` v3+ does handle variants properly—it understands that `flex-col` applies unconditionally while `data-vertical:flex-col` only applies when that variant is active, so they're technically about the same CSS property but in different states.

Since the base has no explicit unconditional flex direction, the user's `flex-col` would add an unconditional column direction, resulting in always being flex-col with the `data-vertical:flex-col` applying redundantly when vertical. That's fine—the `StepperWithPanels` will always be column layout.

Now I need to verify the `StepperItem` components are positioned correctly within their wrapper.  Since `StepperItem` uses `useStepper()` internally to read from context, it doesn't need to be a direct child of `Stepper`—wrapping them in a `<div>` is fine. The `StepperSeparator` also reads context for orientation, and since it's a descendant of `Stepper`, it has access too. Everything checks out, and the `useStepperItem` function isn't causing any TypeScript issues since it's used by `StepperTrigger`.

content.text:
Exactly the right files. Two new files created, two registry files edited — nothing else touched.

Here's a summary of everything that was implemented:

---

## What was done

**4 file operations total — 2 new files created, 2 registry entries added:**

### New file 1: `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx`
A fully custom compound component with no Radix dependencies. Key design:

- **Two context layers**: `StepperContext` (root) holds `value`, `onValueChange`, `orientation`, `steps[]`; `StepperItemContext` (per-item) holds `value`, `isActive`, `isCompleted`, `isPending`
- **Controlled + uncontrolled**: accepts `value`/`onValueChange` for controlled mode, `defaultValue` for uncontrolled (defaults to `steps[0]`)
- **Auto-computed completion**: `isCompleted = steps.indexOf(itemValue) < steps.indexOf(activeValue)` — user just passes the ordered `steps` array
- **Orientation**: `data-orientation` on root + `group/stepper` class; children use `group-data-vertical/stepper:` selectors; `data-horizontal:`/`data-vertical:` on the separator use the project's custom Tailwind variants (from `shadcn/tailwind.css`)
- **State attributes**: `data-active=""`, `data-completed=""`, `data-pending=""` on `StepperItem` and `StepperTrigger` for styling hooks
- **`StepperPanel`**: unmounts when inactive (ideal for wizards)
- **`StepperSeparator`**: reads orientation from context, renders `h-px flex-1` (horizontal) or `w-px min-h-6` (vertical); always `aria-hidden`
- **`StepperTrigger`**: sets `aria-current="step"` when active
- **Exports**: `Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperPanel`, `useStepper`

### New file 2: `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx`
Four demo sections following the `accordion-example.tsx` pattern:

| Example | What it shows |
|---|---|
| **Basic** | Horizontal 3-step stepper with controlled `useState`, step number badges that turn into ✓ when completed |
| **Vertical** | Same steps with `orientation="vertical"`, items show title + description inline |
| **With Content Panels** | Full wizard: `Stepper` in `flex-col` mode wraps a horizontal nav row + `StepperPanel` per step + Back/Next `Button` navigation |
| **In Card** | Horizontal stepper inside a `Card` component |

### Registry insertion: `ui/_registry.ts`
Added `"stepper"` entry alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`.

### Registry insertion: `examples/_registry.ts`
Added `"stepper-example"` entry alphabetically between `spinner-example` and `switch-example`, with `registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button", "card", "example"]`.
